sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal, to absorb, to drink up, to swallow, i.q. Ch.

Piel poet. applied to a horse as it were swallowing the ground in his rapid course. Job 39:24, יְגַמֶּא אֶרֶץ “he swallows the ground,” i.q. he runs away with it. (The same metaphor is of frequent use in Arabic in the verb لهم to swallow up, as التهم الارض; see Schultens ad h. l. and Bochart, Hieroz. i. p. 142-148.)

Hiphil, to give to be drunk or absorbed, Gen 24:17. Hence-
